reviews.author: Mick J
reviews.text: Our pick for favourite beach on the island. Good protection from the easterly winds prevalent in December, a convenient restaurant for food, nice sand, and the bonus of being close to the landing aircraft! (Use Flight Radar website/app for arrival times - the flights are rarely on schedule)

reviews.author: David Haron
reviews.text: The beach was beautiful, it was fun to see the planes coming in. Lots of shade and nice to have some food and drink options available. They charge an admission fee for this beach.

reviews.author: Stasia
reviews.text: What a magnificent place! Absolutely gorgeous beach with almost no people. There are a lot of shadow under the tree, also you can see monkeys nearby. The road by bike from the city is beautiful.

reviews.author: ad bf
reviews.text: Spacious beach close to the airport. Sand is more golden coloured than at other beaches on the island, and the water is clear and cool and a good depth for swimming. Some amazing sunsets here. Entry fee 3k + 4k parking for a scooter and according to the sign you can also camp here for a 30k fee

reviews.author: Pietro R
reviews.text: Wonderful sandy beach just behind the airport. Monkeys tipically wander on the west side toward the Secret Beach passage. Unfortunately waste is not taken care of. Very small admission fee during certain hours.

reviews.author: Tịnh Hoàng
reviews.text: Nice and clean beach. Less windy than bai Dong during wind season. Food is generally expensive as elsewhere in Con Dao but drinks are reasonable.

reviews.author: Gilbert Perron
reviews.text: There is a small fee to get tonthe beach (8k for scooter + 2 person). Mostly clean beach. We were almost alone at the end of the day. Toilets. Expensive sun chair. You can sit on sand for free ;)

reviews.author: Tony Day
reviews.text: A very nice beach, well worth riding over on a scooter. There is an entry fee of 3k. Deckchairs and hammocks can be rented from the café / bar for 30-40k per hour although they didn't restrict to the hour when we were there. I suspect they only do this at busy times or when people bring picnics.

reviews.author: Sian Vincelli
reviews.text: I’m not sure why this beach is so highly rated when there are way nicer beaches in Con Dao. Not only is it super far from the rest of the island, but you have to pay a parking fee AND an “environmental fee” to get in which makes no sense considering how much garbage is along the shores of this beach. The water is not clear and the sand is dirty. If you want to rent a beach chair it’s 30k PER HOUR. Definitely not worth coming here when there are way better beaches on the other side of the island.

reviews.author: Dr Tim Flynn
reviews.text: A nice large beach that bends around the bay near the airport. If you stay there long enough you can see aircraft approach the runway for a landing. I think the Bamboo Air jet arrives near 1500 hours. Keep your phone camera facing west toward the water & aircraft suddenly appear in site. There's a few cafe's along the beach that offer cold drinks...like Tiger beer and that is great. The bay is quite sheltered and the water is usually very clear & calm for swimming safely. To get there you will travel north out of Con Dao town along a scenic and mountainous well paved road. Just make sure you follow to the right at the fork in the road and then turn left on the next wide road to the beach...if not you will be at the airport and have to turn back. Well worth the ride & relax at the beach all day. The sunsets are spectacular as the beach faces west.

reviews.author: Wanda
reviews.text: 4.000 for the scooter + 3.000 per person = 10.000 lost to get on a very very dirty beach full of trash.

reviews.author: Charles Wallace LeBar
reviews.text: Avoid this beach; there are many more that are much better and do not cost you any money. You will get charged to park your scooter, an entrance fee and try to charge for usage of the bathrooms. Definitely the dirtiest beach we experienced.

reviews.author: Lancelot Colas
reviews.text: Nice beach. Not the cleanest ever but definitely cleaner than other on Con Dao. Parking at the entrance for 4k, the entrance is 3k. Not crowed at all. Nice place for sunset. Funny to see the planes land and take-off !

reviews.author: Johannes Krasser
reviews.text: Very nice beach with the possibility to watch airplanes coming in from time to time. It is clean, there are also options to rent a hammock or a chair. A beach you could only dream of in other parts of the world.

reviews.author: Anastasiia
reviews.text: A beautiful beach with the airport right around the corner so can see the planes landing, which is pretty impressive! Watch out for your stuff - some monkeys are very keen on stealing your food and drinks :)

reviews.author: Margreet van Veluw
reviews.text: Nice beach, very quiet and remote. There are a few restaurants, and a public restroom. Nice to see the planes right before they land. Easy to acces because they recently built a new road, just take the main road en 200 meters before the airport, turn left. From there it's just 1 kilometer.

reviews.author: Oleg Buyukli
reviews.text: Best beach in Con Dao with attractions ( low flying arriving planes)

reviews.author: Minh Nhựt
reviews.text: 5k for motorbike parking, 5k for beach access. The water is clear, and the sunset here is great. You could watch planes take off from here.

reviews.author: The MY World
reviews.text: It's nice beach where you an spend whole day. You can see the airplane is landing. It's located near airport. Anyway the water is very clear here.

reviews.author: FrancisP
reviews.text: Very beautiful beach and the amazing thing is that you can have chance to see the airplane landing just above your head. The water is crystal clear. Golden sand beach with some beautiful landscaping

reviews.author: Marius Petkelis
reviews.text: Dam trau beach was the best beach we have been to in Vietnam, it was incredibly quiet and private. The journey leading up to the beach is scenic wonderful scooter ride. The 3 restaurants in the beach are very mediocre so I would recommend to avoid eating there. Getting drinks is fine. The beach is located next to airport so throughout the day there will be planes landing which is an attraction on its own.

reviews.author: catherine ganzleben
reviews.text: Simply stunning, clean clear water, at low tide you can climb over rocks at the left end of the beach to a second beach fringed entirely by tropical forest.

reviews.author: Tran Quoc Trung
reviews.text: Best beach on the island, with so few people on the beach, quiet and clean. Con Dao airport is located nearby the beach so you can catch the moment airplanes landing from here.

reviews.author: Kyle Nguyen
reviews.text: A peaceful beach for you to enjoy and breeze the vitamin sea. There are some local food providers, they can provide basic but nice seafood and offer you free seats on the sand and under the tree (hammock, nilon plastic...)

reviews.author: Han Nguyen
reviews.text: a small beach near the airport, very quiet and clean.

reviews.author: Christof Muehlberg
reviews.text: Good beach. Especially the landing turboprop planes are fun to watch. As they are few, they are no disturbance. The water is clear and the sand fine and white. Perfect beach. Prices in the local restaurant are ok for drinks. Did not check the food, though.

reviews.author: Erika Andrea Carranza Rojas
reviews.text: Beautiful beach! Clean and the most interesting thing is that you can go to a secret beach next to it, the way to go it's a bit risky, so maybe someone could try to improve it but going there was worthy. We used maps me app to discover that beach since you can not even notice it on google maps.

reviews.author: Stephen HcfPorsche
reviews.text: 5000vnd to park motorcycle (4k during weekdays) 3000vnd each to go on the beach 30,000vnd to hire deckchair for 1hr 40,000vnd to hire hammock Small amount of trash on the beach, which should really be picked up regularly if they charge you to go on the beach. Good location to see the aircraft landing. Get the FlightRadar24 app to track flights and see the landing times. Aircraft usually land 10mins earlier than the advertised arrival time. Have to cross a stream, so wear water shoes etc. Best to go before 3pm due to tides.

reviews.author: Ken Machado Sugita
reviews.text: Most recommended beach in Con Dao but sadly not worth the visit. The beach, like others on this beautiful little island, is littered with garbage and no one cares much to clean any of it. A lot of potential to be a fantastic spot to spend the day but unfortunately, tourism infrastructure on the island doesn’t go beyond the hotels and resorts.

reviews.author: Menno van Rosmalen
reviews.text: The most magical place on the already magical Con Dao. Planning to go for just a swim, we ended up spending the whole day here. You will find here anything you need: a long stretch of beautiful beach, clear blue water, a magnificent bay to look at and the amusement of a plane just going over the beach before it lands. Also, there are some eat-drink facilities where you can also find some shade. Definitely go here! If you thought this review was helpful, please like or comment.
